body.title: Correct §2.1 BaseDevice contract description in TheoryOfOperation
body.result: §2.1 updated to show actual 15-method abstract interface; start/stop/self_test noted as non-existent in BaseDevice
body.description:
TheoryOfOperation §2.1 shows a 4-method contract: start/stop/health/self_test. The actual unseen_university/device.py BaseDevice defines 15 abstract methods — health, restart, halt, recovery, who_am_i, requirements, capabilities, comms, interface_version, uptime, startup_errors, logs, update_info, where_and_how, block. start, stop, and self_test are NOT abstract methods.

Across sampled device classes: health is universal; start/stop appear only in discord_bot; self_test only in granny. Lifecycle uniformity is not enforced by the abstract base.

**Affected files:** docs/TheoryOfOperation.md §2.1
**Scope boundary:** IN — update the contract description to match the actual abstract interface; OUT — do not change BaseDevice code or add/remove abstract methods
**Test plan:** After fix, the code snippet in §2.1 matches the abstract methods in unseen_university/device.py
**Completion criteria:** §2.1 code snippet shows the real contract (or documents the gap between aspiration and enforcement)
